response.justification: The candidate is an experienced full-stack and systems software developer with a strong track record in web platforms, low-latency services, and embedded tooling, but has no discernible background in SoC or IP modeling, hardware architecture, AI accelerator design, GPU workload analysis, SIMD/MIMD architectures, or deep neural network optimization. The role demands principal-level expertise at the hardware-software co-design boundary for AI accelerators, and the candidate's profile — centered on TypeScript, Node.js, GIS platforms, and direct-mail automation — does not intersect with these requirements in any meaningful way. Even the AI-adjacent work mentioned (MCP code generators, agentic workflows) operates at the application layer, not the hardware performance layer the role targets.
strengths: Low-latency systems programming experience at Dunnhumby using async I/O, caching, and high-performance databases shows some performance engineering sensibility
Proficiency in lower-level languages (C, C++, DLang) provides a partial foundation for systems-level work
18+ years of continuous technical growth across diverse domains demonstrates adaptability and engineering depth
concerns: Zero experience with SoC or IP modeling, which is a core daily responsibility of the role
No background in GPU architecture, SIMD/MIMD, deep learning workloads, or AI accelerator optimization — the entire domain of the position

Highest degree is a Bachelor's in Computer Science with no evidence of hardware architecture coursework, research, or equivalent industry depth required for principal-level hardware-software co-design

key_factors.factor: No AI/ML hardware or deep learning workload expertise
